repair_candidate.summary: Repair #93113 in place so memory-core status reports active light, REM, and deep dreaming phases without regressing existing deep dreaming details. The contributor branch is editable and narrow, but merge is blocked until the executor fixes the reported check failures, runs a fresh Codex /review, and passes changed checks.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(memory-core): repair active dreaming status phases
repair_candidate.pr_body:
## What Problem This Solves

`openclaw memory status` can report dreaming as off when only light or REM dreaming phases are active. This carries forward the narrow fix from #93113 for #67868.

## Why This Change Was Made

The contributor branch is editable and already contains the focused memory-core implementation and regression tests. Clownfish should repair that branch rather than replace it so @AgentArcLab keeps authorship and review context.

## User Impact

Operators get accurate phase-aware memory dreaming status while preserving the existing deep dreaming details and limits.

## Evidence

Source PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/93113
Linked issue: #67868
Required validation: `pnpm test extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts`, `pnpm check:changed`, then fresh Codex /review before merge.
credit_notes: Preserve contributor credit for @AgentArcLab via source PR https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/93113.
PR body or squash message should include release-note context for the user-visible memory status behavior; do not edit CHANGELOG.md for a normal PR.

apply_actions.title: fix(memory-core): report active dreaming phases in status
